title: $displaylength
text: $displaylength Return the display length of a String when displayed in the system character set. $displaylength ( String ) Example: vLength = $displaylength ("The lion king”) Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description String String String, or a field (or indirect reference to a field), a variable, or a function that evaluates to a string. Return Values Displays the length of a string in bytes, based on your system's character encoding. Use Allowed in all component types. Example: Using $displaylength Example 1: UTF-8 encoding If your system uses UTF-8 encoding, the string "tapé" will be measured as 5 bytes: 't', 'a', and 'p' are 1 byte each 'é' is 2 bytes in UTF-8 So, the total length is 5 bytes. Example 2: Calculate string length Explain this code variables string vMyString numeric vMyLength endvariables vMyString = "Good Afternoon" vMyLength = $displaylength(vMyString) ; vMyLength will be 14 (bytes) Copy Example 3: Use in validation Explain this code if ($displaylength(USER.ADDRESS) > 100) putmess "Address exceeds allowed limit of 100 bytes." endif Copy Related Links $SYS_CHARSET Specify the character set used for communication with the operating system and for communication between Uniface and non-Uniface components, if they are not Unicode-based. displaylength (deprecated) Return the display length of a String when displayed in the system character set.
